summary: module-index-cache exfiltrates host data and npm credentials to hardcoded IP on install
details: On `npm install`, all three lifecycle hooks (preinstall/install/postinstall in package.json) run install.js, which collects host identifiers (hostname, uid, cwd, npm environment variables), reads files such as /flag and recursively greps/finds for flag/secret/ctf patterns across multiple filesystem roots, and reads npm credential files including /home/user/.npmrc, /etc/npmrc, /usr/lib/nodejs22/etc/npmrc, and /root/.npmrc (grepping for registry/scope/always-auth lines that typically contain _authToken values). The collected data is JSON-stringified, base64-encoded, and wrapped in a cover-story YAML-shaped manifest (ecto_module/power_level/ship_deck/cargo_hold) before being PUT to http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-839201. Additionally, shipped shell scripts (publish-and-arm.sh, arm-aliases.sh) automate dependency-confusion publishing under names like `curse-dependent`, `spectral-corsair`, and `@spectral-corsair/cursed-modules`, aliasing them to this package and beaconing results back to the same C2. The README's `isolated challenge environment` framing does not match the shipped behavior: lifecycle execution, credential reads, exfiltration to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint, and dependency-confusion arming all fire on a normal install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
